title: Fortinet Earnings Q4 2025 | Unified SASE-Boom, Rekordmargen und die „Rule of 45“
description: In dieser Folge analysieren wir Fortinets starken Jahresabschluss 2025, bei dem der Umsatz um 15 % auf 1,91 Milliarden US-Dollar und die Billings um 18 % auf 2,37 Milliarden US-Dollar stiegen. Wir beleuchten den massiven Wachstumstreiber Unified SASE, der um 40 % zulegte, sowie CEO Ken Xies Strategie zur Konvergenz von Networking und Security. Zudem diskutieren wir die beeindruckende Non-GAAP-Betriebsmarge von 37 %, das zum sechsten Mal in Folge übertroffene „Rule of 45“-Ziel und die Erweiterung

title: Defending Your Cyber Systems and Your Mental Attack Surface with Chris Hughes
description:
When your firewall forgets to buckle up, the crash doesn’t happen in the network first, it happens in your blindspots.

In this episode, Ron is joined by returning guest Chris Hughes, Co-Founder of Aquia and host of the Resilient Cyber podcast. Chris helps reframe vulnerability work as exposure management, connect technical risk to human resilience, and break down the scoring and runtime tools security teams actually need today. Expect clear takeaways on EPSS, reachability analysis, ADR, AI’s do

title: Quarterly Portfolio Review: $FTNT, $RACE, $UNP
description:
In this episode of Stock Spotlight, I walk through a quarterly portfolio review across three companies I own: Fortinet, Ferrari and Union Pacific.

For each company, I break down:
• The original investment thesis
• Key KPIs and how they’re trending
• What the market is focused on right now
• My forward outlook
• A simple green / yellow / red rating

The goal is simple: track whether execution still supports the underwriting.

title: Fortinet Crashes 28% — Smart Buy or Total Trap? FTNT stock
show: Am I Catching a Falling Knife?
description: Fortinet (FTNT) just tanked 28% — but is this cybersecurity giant a hidden gem or a value trap? In this episode of Am I Catching a Falling Knife?, Dave breaks down the real reasons behind the drop: slowing service revenue, SASE cannibalization fears, and analyst downgrades.
